summary: The test suite failed because two assertions in test.test_tcl failed: test_eval_surrogates_in_result and test_evalfile_surrogates_in_result. Both expected a surrogate-escaped string but received a differently decoded string.
root_cause: Two tests in test.test_tcl failed due to an assertion mismatch: the tests expected '<\ud83d\udcbb>' (surrogate-escaped representation) but the actual Tcl result was '<ðŸ’»>'.

evidence.reason: Shows the specific test that failed.
Shows the second failing test in the same test file.
The assertion in the test expects the surrogate-escaped form '<\ud83d\udcbb>' / '<\U0001f4bb>'.
The assertion failure message shows the expected surrogate-escaped string ('<\ud83d\udcbb>') does not match the actual returned string ('<ðŸ’»>').
Indicates the Tcl/Tk version in use (useful because the failure relates to Tcl string results).
Summary showing that the test_tcl test suite failed.

suggested_fix: Reproduce the two failing tests locally (Lib\test\test_tcl.py) and inspect the raw bytes returned by tcl.eval to determine how Tcl/Tk is encoding the result. Verify the Tcl/Tk build/version (externals\tcltk-9.0.3.0-unsigned\amd64) and the code path that decodes Tcl strings into Python (ensure surrogate escapes are preserved if the test expects them). Adjust the decoding behavior or test expectation accordingly and re-run the failing tests.
